LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT GREGORY MCNULTY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS ALL OF THE ELEMENTS. (JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA CIVIL JURY INSTRUCTION ("CACI") 4103; CROSS-COMPLAINT, 6, 14, 21-23.) AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS ALL OF THE ELEMENTS. (THRIFTY PAYLESS, INC. V. AMERICAN AT BRAND, LLC (2013) 218 CAL.APP.4TH 1230, 1239; CROSS-COMPLAINT, 9, 12, 13, 15-17, 34-36.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS ALL OF THE ELEMENTS. (THRIFTY PAYLESS, INC. V. AMERICAN AT BRAND, LLC (2013) 218 CAL.APP.4TH 1230, 1239; CROSS-COMPLAINT, 13, 15-17, 34-36.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2014 Set For Trial On Sep-15-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT GREGORY MCNULTY's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS Overruled. As to the first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, the Cross-Complaint adequately pleads all of the elements. (Judicial Council of California Civil Jury Instruction ("CACI") 4103; Cross-Complaint, 6, 14, 21-23.) As to the second cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, the Cross-Complaint adequately pleads all of the elements. (Thrifty Payless, Inc. v. American at Brand, LLC (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1230, 1239; Cross-Complaint, 9, 12, 13, 15-17, 34-36.) As to the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, the Cross-Complaint adequately pleads all of the elements. (Thrifty Payless, Inc. v. American at Brand, LLC (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1230, 1239; Cross-Complaint, 13, 15-17, 34-36.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
